page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per core ratio. It runs on the x86_64 architecture, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. This configuration consists of 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor features 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, its multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor results, with read-only large block bandwidth at 31.35 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.01 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform at average levels. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing rank in the bottom 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the n2d-highmem-2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, which provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring more RAM than compute power. However, the low multi-core performance and poor database operations score suggest it is not suited for heavy database workloads or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound tasks, single-threaded processing, basic text processing, or small-scale LLM text generation using compact models, where high memory capacity per core is required without intensive parallel CPU demands.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 1 physical core with 2 threads and 32 MB of L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng, contrasted by weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing perform at average levels, while parallelized workloads and database operations show limitations. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for lightweight memory-intensive applications, single-threaded processing, and small-scale text generation tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight memory-bound tasks and single-threaded processing
